reasoning: The U.S. collected $82.2 billion in customs duties in FY 2024. FY 2025 runs from October 2024 to September 2025. The Trump administration took office in late January 2025, meaning any tariff increases could only impact the final ~8 months of the fiscal year. By April 2025, customs receipts had exceeded expectations by only $15 billion. To reach $500 billion, the U.S. would need to collect over $400 billion in the remaining 5 months of the fiscal year (over $80B per month). Given total U.S. monthly imports are around $300-$350 billion, this would mathematically require an average effective tariff rate in excess of 25% on all goods applied immediately, which did not happen. Revenues will undoubtedly fall far short of the $500b bracket.
batch_queries: Will the U.S. collect between $500b and $1t in revenue in 2025?
